prompts.title: Pre-Arrival WhatsApp Welcome for Goa Leisure Guest
prompts.prompt: Act as a guest experience manager with 10 years at IHCL and Marriott India. Write a pre-arrival WhatsApp message (under 110 words) for a 2-night Goa leisure guest checking in tomorrow at our 90-room beach resort. The message should: (1) confirm booking details (name, dates, room category, MAP plan, airport transfer slot), (2) offer 3 personalisation choices (welcome drink — kokum / king coconut / champagne, pillow type — soft / firm, dietary preference — veg / non-veg / jain), (3) share a 1-link to a curated Goa-experience menu (sunset cruise, Spice plantation, dolphin trip), (4) provide a single point of contact (name + designation + WhatsApp number for instant queries), (5) end with a warm, brand-appropriate sign-off. Format as a copy-paste ready WhatsApp template plus an internal CRM tag list. Constraints: tone warm Indian English; avoid corporate jargon; respect DPDP consent — link to privacy notice; trigger send at 10 AM IST day prior.

prompts.title: Tripadvisor 4-Star Review Response Template
prompts.prompt: Act as the Front Office Manager at a 120-room business hotel in Bengaluru's Whitefield. I will paste a 4-star Tripadvisor review where the guest praises rooms and breakfast but complains about (a) slow check-in (35 minutes wait), and (b) noisy AC. Write a public response (under 120 words) that: (1) thanks the guest by first name, (2) acknowledges what they loved without sounding rehearsed, (3) owns the wait-time issue with a one-line specific commitment (e.g., kiosk express check-in for return guests), (4) explains AC investigation in plain English (technical team will visit, not 'we have noted your feedback'), (5) invites them back with a personal manager contact line, (6) signs off with name + designation. Format as a single paragraph optimised for the Tripadvisor character cap. Constraints: avoid 'sincere apologies' clichés; do not promise a free upgrade publicly; keep it specific so other readers see action, not platitudes.

prompts.title: Service Recovery Script for Room-Not-Ready
prompts.prompt: Act as a Duty Manager with 12 years at Indian 5-star hotels. Write a service recovery script for a guest arriving at 2 PM (standard check-in 3 PM) when the room is not ready. The guest is a corporate traveller from Mumbai with a 4 PM client meeting in our hotel. Cover: (1) front-desk greeting and explanation (under 30 seconds, no excuses), (2) immediate value offered (use of executive lounge / shower facility / cloak room with refresh kit), (3) a complimentary in-room amenity for the inconvenience (fruit platter / handwritten note from GM / spa voucher — pick one, justify), (4) clear time commitment with a fallback (room ready by X; if not, free upgrade), (5) follow-up cadence post check-in (call from FOM at 5 PM to confirm meeting went well), (6) internal tagging in PMS (Opera / IDS Next) for next visit prep. Format as 3 spoken-line scripts (warm / firm / VIP variants) + a checklist for the agent. Constraints: no over-promising compensation; align with brand SOP; retain dignity of the guest in front of others in lobby.

prompts.title: Indian Wedding Banquet Event Order (BEO)
prompts.prompt: Act as a Banquet Sales Manager with 14 years at Indian luxury hotels (Taj, Leela, ITC). Build a complete Banquet Event Order (BEO) for a 600-pax Indian wedding sangeet at our 25,000 sqft pillar-less ballroom in Udaipur. Event details: 7 PM-1 AM, live band, dance floor, vegetarian + select non-veg, full bar, plated 5-course + 2 live counters. BEO should cover: (1) timeline minute-by-minute (decor in, sound check, guests in, performances, dinner, cake, send-off), (2) F&B menu with allergen flags + jain options, (3) bar plan (premium IMFL + custom mocktails + dry-day check), (4) AV brief (PA, lighting, LED wall), (5) staffing matrix (banquet captains, waiters, bar staff, sound), (6) decor coordination (florist, mandap, photography zone), (7) compliance (excise, Police NOC, fire NOC, PPL/IPRS music license, GST 5/18%), (8) financials (guarantee covers, attrition clause, payment milestones, advance), (9) signatures + handover to ops. Format as a 3-page printable BEO. Constraints: include Indian dietary nuances (jain, swaminarayan, halal); cap 5% wastage; honour 'no-tip-asking' policy.

prompts.title: Upselling Script at Check-In Counter
prompts.prompt: Act as a Front Office trainer with 9 years across mid-scale Indian hotels (Lemon Tree, Ginger, Holiday Inn). Write a check-in counter upsell script for our 140-room business hotel in Hyderabad. Standard rate ₹4,500. We want to upsell to premium room (+₹900) or club room (+₹1,800 with lounge access, breakfast, evening cocktails, late check-out). Script should: (1) ask 2 qualifying questions (purpose of stay, business / leisure mix), (2) present 2 upgrade tiers with concrete value framing (per benefit, not just price), (3) handle 3 common objections (too expensive / didn't pre-book / breakfast already included), (4) include a 'walk-away' graceful close, (5) capture upsell tracking data in PMS for monthly review, (6) tie to incentive (₹50 per successful upsell to FO agent). Format as a dialogue tree + objection matrix. Constraints: no pressure tactics — Indian guests detect and resent; preserve repeat-guest goodwill; align with internal CRM tags.

prompts.title: OTA Booking Cancellation Win-Back Email
prompts.prompt: Act as an e-commerce manager with 8 years across Indian hospitality OTAs (MakeMyTrip, Goibibo, Booking.com). Write a 110-word win-back email triggered when a guest cancels a confirmed booking at our 80-room boutique hotel in Pondicherry. Email should: (1) acknowledge the cancellation without guilt-tripping, (2) offer 3 future-stay options (date-flexible voucher 10% off, free upgrade for the same dates, cabbed pickup from Chennai airport included), (3) ask one optional reason (date change / price / travel postponed) via a single-click feedback link, (4) protect direct-booking margin by linking to the hotel's website (15% discount vs OTA price), (5) sign with the GM's name. Provide subject line variants (3) optimised for Indian inbox. Format as a ready-to-send email with HTML and plain-text alt. Constraints: comply with DPDP consent for CRM mailing; do not message after 3 cancellations to avoid harassment perception; tone is empathetic not desperate.

prompts.title: Daily Revenue Manager Dashboard Brief
prompts.prompt: Act as Director of Revenue with 12 years across Indian hotel chains. Brief a junior revenue manager on the daily 9 AM dashboard review for a 250-room hotel in Mumbai BKC. Cover: (1) the 7 metrics they must check first thing — pickup vs same-day-last-week, OTA vs direct mix, ADR by segment (corporate / leisure / MICE), pace next 30/60/90, comp set rate parity (using STR / Hotelogix), forecast vs actual, online review velocity, (2) typical anomalies and what to do (sudden drop in pickup, OTA undercut, group block release date), (3) 3 daily decisions (rate fence, restriction tweak, allotment) with a 5-minute decision template, (4) 1 weekly Founder/GM update format, (5) data sources and refresh times (PMS, CRS, OTA Channel Manager, GA4 for direct site). Format as an SOP doc + decision flowchart. Constraints: avoid generic 'check the report' instructions; tie every metric to one rate decision; keep within 10-minute morning routine.

prompts.title: Housekeeping Quality Audit Checklist
prompts.prompt: Act as an Executive Housekeeper with 18 years across Oberoi, Hyatt and Indian luxury hotels. Build a 50-point room quality audit checklist for a 5-star room at our hotel in Jaipur. Cover: (1) entrance and door (2 points), (2) bedroom (15 points: bedding, pillows, linen, dust, lamps, side tables), (3) bathroom (12 points: hygiene, amenities, water pressure, towels), (4) wardrobe (5 points), (5) tea-coffee setup (4 points), (6) windows + curtains (3 points), (7) flooring + carpet (3 points), (8) air quality + smell (3 points), (9) tech (TV, IPTV, Wi-Fi, room phone — 3 points). For each point: (a) what to inspect, (b) pass/fail criteria with measurable threshold, (c) common defects + corrective action, (d) photo evidence requirement. Format as a printable A4 checklist + a digital tablet-ready JSON for housekeeping app. Constraints: align with brand standard (LQA / Forbes / brand-specific); allow Hindi/regional language toggle for staff; tie to monthly bonus pool.

prompts.title: Crisis Communication for Power Outage
prompts.prompt: Act as a General Manager with 15 years at Indian luxury hotels. Draft a 3-tier crisis communication plan for a 4-hour power outage at our 200-room hotel in Cochin during peak season. Tiers: (T1) within first 30 mins, (T2) at 90 mins, (T3) restoration. Each tier has: (1) front-desk script (verbal to in-house guests), (2) WhatsApp blast (under 60 words, IST timestamps), (3) public address (recorded loudspeaker bilingual), (4) social media posting (Twitter/X, Insta story, FB post — same core, channel-tuned), (5) press / media holding statement (in case of escalation), (6) post-event apology + recovery offer (per category — premium / club / suite). Also include: internal team comms (engineering, F&B, FO, security), insurance documentation steps, and a debrief framework. Format as a 1-page crisis protocol. Constraints: never blame the utility provider publicly; comply with DGCA-equivalent disclosure if guests are flying out; preserve brand calm.

prompts.title: MICE Event RFP Response Letter
prompts.prompt: Act as Director of Sales (MICE) with 11 years at Indian conference hotels. I will paste a 3-day, 250-pax pharmaceutical conference RFP (residential, all meals, AV, themed dinners, transport, GST). Write the formal RFP response letter (450 words) plus an attached 6-tab proposal outline (rooming, F&B menus, AV, themed nights, transport, financials). The response letter should: (1) demonstrate understanding of the pharma context (CDSCO, MR engagement, regulatory disclosure), (2) lead with 2 differentiators (outdoor breakaway zones, on-site healthcare doctor on call), (3) clearly state pricing model (per pax all-inclusive vs broken into rooms / F&B / AV), (4) name 3 reference clients in the same sector (with permission), (5) commit to a site-visit invite within 7 days, (6) sign off with DOSM name + direct phone + WhatsApp. Format as a Word-ready letter + outline. Constraints: include MEA / Pharmacovigilance compliance reassurance; price band ₹6,500-₹9,500 per pax/day; payment milestones 25-50-25.

prompts.title: Wellness Spa Package Design and Pricing
prompts.prompt: Act as Spa Director with 10 years at Indian wellness resorts (CGH Earth, Niraamaya, Ananda). Design 3 signature spa packages for our 60-room wellness retreat in Coorg. Packages: (a) 'Coorg Coffee Glow' single therapy 60-min, (b) 'Western Ghats Reset' 3-therapy half-day, (c) 'Forest Mind' 2-day silent retreat. For each: (1) therapy menu with Ayurvedic + International blends, (2) ingredients sourced locally (Coorg coffee bean exfoliant, sandalwood paste from Mysore, eucalyptus from Wayanad), (3) duration and price (INR + USD for inbound tourists), (4) inclusions (refreshments, robes, post-therapy refreshment), (5) therapist labour cost + product cost + room utilisation = margin band, (6) marketing one-liner + Insta caption. Provide bookable copy + an internal P&L per package. Constraints: AYUSH compliance for Ayurvedic therapy claims; minimum 65% margin on each package; package names must be evocative without medical claims.

prompts.title: Long-Stay Corporate Rate Negotiation
prompts.prompt: Act as Director of Sales with 13 years at Indian business hotels. Draft a tiered LRA (long-stay rate agreement) negotiation framework for a corporate client (TCS, 18,000 employee transfers/year between Bengaluru and Pune). Provide: (1) data the corporate must share to qualify (annual room nights forecast, lead time, peak demand calendar, current vendors), (2) rate tiers based on annual commitment (50, 200, 500, 1000+ nights) with progressive perks (free breakfast, lounge access, late check-out, complimentary gym, suite upgrade after N stays), (3) value-adds non-rate (priority allocation, dedicated account manager, monthly business review), (4) cancellation and credit terms (30-day SLA, 60-day net), (5) clauses (force-majeure, blackout dates, GST + TDS), (6) renewal review SLA. Format as a 2-page proposal + negotiation cheat-sheet. Constraints: protect own ADR by capping discount at 22% to LRA tier 4; ensure compliance with TCS vendor onboarding doc; handshake clause with quarterly rate review.

prompts.title: F&B Menu Refresh for All-Day Dining
prompts.prompt: Act as F&B Manager with 12 years at Indian 5-star all-day dining outlets. Lead a quarterly menu refresh for our 110-cover all-day at our hotel in Chennai. Cover: (1) menu engineering matrix (popularity vs profitability — stars / plough horses / puzzles / dogs), (2) data sources (POS sales mix, plate cost, guest comments), (3) 6 dish replacements with rationale (1-line decision logic per dish), (4) 4 new dish introductions (one each: South Indian comfort, Pan-Asian, healthy / vegan, indulgent dessert) with allergen + jain tags, (5) re-photography brief (in-house or external photographer), (6) staff training plan (table-side description, allergen awareness, paired beverage), (7) launch plan (soft for 7 days, full launch on a Friday), (8) success KPI (mix shift target, cover increase, guest comment Insta volume). Format as a refresh brief + decision matrix. Constraints: maintain food cost band 30-33%; one signature breakfast item must remain; align with Hindu calendar for festive specials.

prompts.title: Loyalty Program Tier Letter to Guests
prompts.prompt: Act as Loyalty Program Manager with 9 years at Indian hotel groups (Taj NeuPass, Marriott Bonvoy India). Write a 220-word letter announcing a guest's promotion from Silver to Gold tier in our hotel chain's loyalty programme. Letter should: (1) congratulate by first name with a specific personalisation (e.g., 'we noticed your stays at our Goa, Jaipur and Kerala properties this year'), (2) list the 6 new benefits unlocked (room upgrade subject to availability, lounge access, late check-out, welcome amenity, F&B 15% off, anniversary gift on first stay), (3) explain how to redeem (mobile app + loyalty desk), (4) tease one bigger benefit at next tier (Platinum: free night anniversary), (5) sign with handwritten-style signature of the Loyalty VP, (6) include a personalised offer code valid 90 days (₹2,000 off F&B at any property). Format as a printable PDF + email HTML. Constraints: DPDP-aligned consent reaffirmation footer; multilingual greeting (English + Hindi salutation); avoid 'Dear valued guest' clichés.

prompts.title: Pre-Opening Hotel Checklist (T-90 to T-0)
prompts.prompt: Act as a hotel pre-opening consultant with 15 years across Indian openings. Build a 90-day pre-opening checklist for our 180-room midscale hotel launch in Lucknow. Workstreams: (1) licenses (FSSAI, Excise, Police NOC, Fire NOC, ESI, EPF, Trade, Lift, Music IPRS/PPL, GST, IATA), (2) hiring (department-wise headcount, training calendar, payroll go-live), (3) F&B (kitchen handover, supplier onboarding, soft-launch menu), (4) Revenue (PMS Opera/IDS go-live, OTA channel manager onboarding, GDS code), (5) Marketing (brand launch plan, Insta seeding, Google My Business, local PR), (6) Engineering (utilities, BMS handover, asset register), (7) Soft launch (3 weekends with 30-50-70% occupancy stress tests), (8) Grand opening day plan. Format as a 90-day milestone Gantt + accountability table (RACI). Constraints: align with Indian local-body inspection cycles (Nagar Nigam); pre-opening budget cap ₹3.6 Cr; protect asset SLA with developer contract.

prompts.title: Distress Inventory Sale Email to Travel Agents
prompts.prompt: Act as Director of Sales with 10 years at Indian leisure hotels. Draft a 130-word email to 40 select travel-agent partners selling distress inventory: 60 room nights at our Jodhpur palace hotel for the next 14 days. Email should: (1) lead with the offer headline (₹X net rate vs published ₹Y), (2) specify validity, blackout dates, and inclusions (breakfast + 1 guided heritage walk), (3) commission protection (10% off-published, agent retains all margin above), (4) booking SOP (block via WhatsApp, confirm by email, payment via NEFT/UPI, vouchers within 24 hrs), (5) urgency (sale closes at 6 PM IST tomorrow), (6) personal sign-off with phone + WhatsApp. Provide 3 subject line A/B options. Format as a ready-to-send mail. Constraints: exclude OTA partners (rate parity risk); add a 'do-not-public-post' line; align with existing TAFI/IATO partner ranks.

prompts.title: Banquet Hall AV Brief for Tech Conference
prompts.prompt: Act as MICE Operations Manager with 9 years at Indian convention hotels. Build the AV brief for a 500-pax tech conference at our 12,000 sqft pillar-less ballroom in Hyderabad HITEC City. Day-long, 1 keynote + 4 panels + 8 breakouts + networking dinner. Cover: (1) main hall AV (LED wall pixel pitch, line-array PA, lapel + handheld mics, presenter laptop interface, comfort monitors, simultaneous interpretation booth EN/HI), (2) breakout rooms (projector / 65-inch LED, wireless mic, click-share), (3) networking dinner sound (DJ + emcee, dance-floor lighting), (4) live-streaming + recording brief (4 camera, vMix), (5) AV labour matrix (engineers, runners, FOH operator), (6) backup plan (UPS, generator, redundant mic), (7) cost estimate INR (in-house vs vendor mix), (8) tech rehearsal schedule. Format as an AV BoM + setup timeline. Constraints: ensure CDSCO-compliant if pharma keynote; comply with PPL/IPRS music licensing for DJ set; vendor empanelment list maintained.

prompts.title: Daily Stand-up Talk Track for HOD Meeting
prompts.prompt: Act as the General Manager of a 220-room hotel. Build the talk track for the 10 AM daily HOD stand-up. Attendees: FOM, EH, F&B, Sales, Revenue, Engineering, HR, Security. Cover: (1) yesterday recap (key arrivals/departures, in-house guest VIP review, complaint log, occupancy + ADR vs forecast), (2) today (arrivals notable, group movement, banquet events, F&B reservations, special requests), (3) tomorrow look-ahead (group block check, corporate rate verification, pre-arrival readiness), (4) escalations (guest issues, OOO rooms, staff absence, supplier delivery), (5) one strategic item (monthly theme — e.g., guest experience week, sustainability week), (6) action item recap with owner + due time. Format as a 12-minute meeting agenda + a 1-page talk track. Constraints: cap meeting at 12 minutes; rotating focus departments; capture commitments in a shared sheet visible to GM; keep tone empowering not punitive.

prompts.title: Food Safety + Hygiene Audit (FSSAI + HACCP)
prompts.prompt: Act as Food Safety Supervisor with 11 years across Indian luxury and mid-scale hotels. Build a quarterly food safety audit framework for our hotel kitchen (banquet + 3 restaurants + 24-hr in-room dining). Cover: (1) regulatory baseline (FSSAI Schedule 4 Part II, HACCP, NABH where applicable), (2) audit checklist by area (receiving, dry stores, walk-in chillers, prep zones, cooking line, plating, banquet hold, in-room delivery, dishwash, garbage), (3) CCP records (temperature logs, allergen control, swab tests, water quality), (4) staff training compliance (medical fitness annual, hygiene certificate), (5) supplier compliance (FSSAI license + invoice trail), (6) non-conformance grading (minor / major / critical) with corrective action SLA, (7) management review trigger thresholds, (8) external auditor pre-audit drill checklist. Format as a 4-page audit framework + scorecard. Constraints: align with HACCP 12-step plan; document retention 6 months minimum; monthly staff brief poster in Hindi/English.

prompts.title: Wedding Site Inspection Tour Script
prompts.prompt: Act as Wedding Sales Manager with 8 years at Indian destination hotels (Udaipur, Jaipur, Goa). Write a 60-minute site inspection tour script for a wedding family visiting our hotel. The family: bride's father (decision maker), bride (lifestyle-led), groom's mother (traditional), wedding planner (industry-savvy). Tour covers: (1) lobby greeting + welcome drinks, (2) ceremony lawn + mandap setup possibility, (3) sangeet / cocktail venue + LED wall demo, (4) wedding banquet hall + table setup demo, (5) bridal suite + getting-ready room, (6) family room block tour + family suites, (7) cuisine tasting (lunch with curated 5 dishes), (8) wrap-up at GM's office for tentative date hold + commercial discussion. For each stop: (a) talking points tailored to each persona, (b) photo op moment, (c) personalised small touches (flowers in bride's favourite colour, mocktail named after the couple), (d) handover to next staff. Format as a minute-by-minute script. Constraints: no hard sell at any point; let family observe other weddings only with privacy intact; GM personally meets at start + close.

prompts.title: Sustainability Initiative Communication
prompts.prompt: Act as Sustainability Manager with 7 years at Indian green hotels (CGH Earth, ITC Hotels). Draft a 1-page guest-facing communication summarising our hotel's monsoon-quarter sustainability initiatives at our 110-room property in Munnar. Initiatives: (1) 100% rainwater harvesting cover, (2) solar 40% of power, (3) zero-single-use-plastic in rooms (bottled water replaced by glass), (4) farm-to-table 60% F&B sourcing within 80 km, (5) employee community uplift (school adoption, mid-day meal). Communication should: (1) lead with one specific impact metric (e.g., '14 lakh litres of rainwater saved'), (2) describe each initiative in 30 words, (3) invite the guest to opt-in (towel reuse card, opt-out of housekeeping linen change), (4) link to a transparency page (2-pager PDF), (5) sign with the GM and Sustainability Manager. Format as a tabletop card + an in-app push notification version. Constraints: avoid greenwash language; quantify every claim; align with EarthCheck / GRIHA framework.

prompts.title: Group Block Management Conflict Email
prompts.prompt: Act as Group Reservations Manager with 9 years at Indian convention hotels. Two corporate groups have requested overlapping 80-room blocks for the same week (TCS leadership offsite vs Infosys managing-director conclave). Draft an email to TCS apologising for unavailability and proposing 3 mitigations. Email should: (1) acknowledge their priority status as 1,200-night-a-year client without name-dropping the conflict, (2) explain inventory pressure factually but briefly, (3) offer 3 mitigations (a sister property at 18% off, dates 1 week earlier with full block confirmed, hybrid plan with 60 rooms here + 20 at sister), (4) commit a specific account-manager call within 24 hours, (5) sign off warmly with DOSM contact. Provide a parallel internal note to Revenue and FOM aligning on the chosen mitigation. Format as 2 ready-to-send messages (external + internal). Constraints: never disclose the conflicting client; protect TCS goodwill above short-term ADR; document decision rationale for audit.

prompts.title: Spa Cancellation Refund Policy Letter
prompts.prompt: Act as Front Office Manager with 10 years across Indian wellness resorts. Draft a 180-word refund policy notification for a spa booking cancellation under 24 hours. Guest had booked a 90-min couples massage (₹8,500) at our wellness spa in Rishikesh for tomorrow 11 AM, cancelled at 9 PM tonight. Policy: 50% retention for under-24-hour cancellations. Letter should: (1) acknowledge the cancellation with empathy, (2) state the policy clearly with the retained amount, (3) offer a discretionary alternative (date-flexible voucher 100% transferable for 6 months), (4) explain the rationale briefly (therapist deployment), (5) provide a clear next-step (auto-refund timing on the original payment method 7-10 days), (6) close with manager contact + warm sign-off. Format as a ready-to-send email + WhatsApp short version (under 60 words). Constraints: comply with the consumer protection norms; do not blame guest; show flexibility when guest seems genuinely apologetic.

prompts.title: Seasonal Forecast Memo to Owner Investors
prompts.prompt: Act as the General Manager + Hotel Asset team writing the quarterly forecast memo to hotel owner-investors. Property: 250-room luxury hotel in Mumbai BKC. Period: Oct-Dec quarter. Memo should cover: (1) macro context (Indian travel demand, BKC corporate calendar, festive impact), (2) booking-on-the-books pace + pickup velocity vs LY, (3) revenue forecast with high / base / low scenarios + key sensitivities, (4) cost forecast (food, payroll, utilities, capex), (5) EBITDA vs budget, (6) risks (weather, election impact, group cancellations), (7) opportunity initiatives (Diwali experience packaging, year-end gala, NYE residential), (8) capex asks for next quarter (with payback, IRR), (9) ask from owners (decisions / approvals). Format as a 3-page memo + financial table. Constraints: numbers in ₹ Cr, decimals to 1; tone candid not optimistic; flag IND-AS revenue recognition where applicable; benchmark vs comp set (J.W. Marriott, Trident, ITC Maratha).

prompts.title: Repeat Guest Recognition Personalization Brief
prompts.prompt: Act as Guest Relations Manager with 8 years across IHCL and Marriott India. Build a recognition brief for a returning guest at our 150-room luxury hotel in Bengaluru. Guest profile: 6 stays in last 18 months, prefers room facing east (sunrise), allergic to peanuts, anniversary on Nov 14, wife joins on this trip, prefers Earl Grey tea, jogs at 6 AM. Brief should produce: (1) pre-arrival room set-up note (specific arrangements per the profile), (2) handwritten welcome card text from GM (60 words), (3) anniversary surprise plan (a non-cliche idea — bespoke morning jog map of the hotel grounds with a photographer waiting at sunrise), (4) F&B personalisation (peanut-free menu pre-flagged), (5) departure gift (locally crafted item under ₹1,500), (6) PMS profile update + sharing across sister properties, (7) post-stay follow-up nudge with photos. Format as a personalisation runbook + handover checklist for staff. Constraints: respect DPDP consent for data sharing across sister properties; avoid being intrusive; staff training in noticing-without-staring.
